title: Red Sox win 7th straight game just hours after landing in New York
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Red Sox won 6-2 against the Mets, marking their seventh straight win and improving their record to 44-48.
Sonny Gray pitched six innings, leading the Red Sox to victory with only one run allowed and three strikeouts.
Anthony Seigler's two-run home run in the seventh inning helped extend Boston's lead, continuing his impressive performance.

title: Christian Gonzalez responds to report that he isn’t physical enough for Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Christian Gonzalez addressed a report by Greg Bedard criticizing his physicality as a Patriots corner.
Despite claims, Gonzalez's tackling stats show comparable performance to other top NFL corners.
Gonzalez leads a strengthened Patriots secondary with the addition of veterans like Kevin Byard.

title: Here’s who experts have the Red Sox selecting with the No. 20 pick in the 2026 MLB Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox hold the No. 20 overall pick in the 2026 MLB Draft set to start Saturday.
Experts give varying predictions for Red Sox's first pick: Chase Brunson, Taylor Rabe, Bo Lowrance, Ace Reese.
The consensus leans towards either selecting a college right-handed pitcher or a potential power-hitting outfielder.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Little House on the Prairie' adaptation is now available for streaming on Netflix.
'The Long Walk' and 'Project Hail Mary' are new film releases streaming on HBO Max and Prime Video, respectively.
"The Five-Star Weekend," filmed on Nantucket, stars Jennifer Garner and is available on Peacock.

title: A 4th Red Sox player has been named a 2026 MLB All-Star
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ceddanne Rafaela named to the 2026 MLB All-Star team, replacing injured Aaron Judge.
Rafaela is the fourth Red Sox player to be named an All-Star this year.
The 25-year-old outfielder has a .283 batting average with eight home runs in 87 games this season.

title: Joey Chestnut to compete in inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Joey Chestnut to compete in the first Saugus Wing Eating Championship on July 11 at The Brook Casino.
Geoffrey Esper, holder of the all-time wing eating record, will face Joey Chestnut in the competition.
The event, featuring Kowloon's famous Saugus Wings, is free and open to those aged 18 and over.

title: France-Morocco was a fitting end to a spectacular World Cup in Boston
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: France defeated Morocco 2-0 in Foxborough, ending Boston's hosting of the 2026 World Cup.
France's Kylian Mbappé scored his eighth tournament goal, helping secure the victory despite an early penalty save.
Boston's hosting of the World Cup featured seven matches and drew global attention, boosting local tourism.

title: Gloucester woman accused of plotting to kill childhood friend using WD-40
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Phyllis Palazzola accused of using a WD-40-soaked rag in an attempted murder of Marianna DiMercurio.
The attack allegedly stemmed from a $160,000 debt Palazzola owed DiMercurio from a previous insurance fraud case.
Palazzola faces felony charges including attempted murder, with arraignment set for July 21 in Essex Superior Court.

title: How a Waltham barber became France’s go-to during the World Cup
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Coco Fernandez of Waltham's Mobar became France's national team's barber after a WhatsApp message connected him to top players.
Fernandez began cutting hair for Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé during France's visit to Boston for a match against Brazil in March.
Fernandez continues to serve as France's and Morocco's national teams' barber as they use Boston as a World Cup base.

title: Red Sox face lengthy travel issues ahead of series vs. Mets
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Boston Red Sox faced a 17-hour travel delay leaving Chicago for a series against the New York Mets.
The team's flight from Chicago was delayed due to a broken tug and a cockpit light issue.
The MLB postponed the Red Sox-Mets game start to 7:50 p.m. ET to accommodate the team's late arrival.
